1734 POINT I/O - Denial of Service via Malformed Inputs on CIP Object
Advisory ID:
SD1779
Published Date:
July 14, 2026
Last Updated:
July 14, 2026
Revision Number:
1.0
Known Exploited Vulnerability (KEV):
No
Corrected:
No
Workaround:
Yes
CVE IDs
CVE-2026-10573
Downloads
The following link(s) provide the security advisory in
Vulnerability Exploitability Exchange
format:
JSON
Summary
The security of our products is important to us as your industrial automation supplier. This security issue was found internally during routine testing and is being reported based on our commitment to customer transparency and improvement of all business environments.
Product Description
POINT I/O™ 8 Point Digital Output Module
Affected Products and Solution
Affected Product
CVE
Affected Firmware Version
Corrected in Firmware Version
Affected Catalog Numbers
1734 POINT I/O
TM
CVE-2026-10573
3.023
N/A
Users are recommended to migrate to 5034-OB8
1734-OB8
Security Issue Details for CVE-2026-10573
Category
Details
CVE ID
CVE-2026-10573
Impact
A denial-of-service security issue exists in 1734 POINT I/O™ module. The security issue stems from improper handling of crafted CIP messages, which can cause the module to enter a faulted state. A restart is required to recover.
CVSS 3.1 Base Score
7.5/10
CVSS 4.0 Base Score
8.7/10
CWE
CWE-770: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
Known Exploited Vulnerability
No
Mitigations and Workarounds
Customers using the affected software, who are not able to upgrade to one of the corrected versions, should use our
security best practices
.
Revision History
Revision
Date
Description
1.0
7/14/26
Initial release
Glossary:
·
Denial of Service:
A malicious attempt to disrupt the normal functioning of a targeted server, service, or network by overwhelming it with a flood of traffic or triggering a crash.
·
CIP (Common Industrial Protocol):
Common Industrial Protocol (CIP) is a common communication standard that is widely used in industrial automation. Comprises a series of protocols for communication between different devices and systems in automation technology.
·
Module:
A self-contained unit within a system that performs a specific function and can operate independently or as part of a larger system.

Contact Us | Rockwell Automation | US.
